about.headline:
Why
IRONMAP?
about.p1: I grew up in a small town. Just under ten thousand people. One square, a few pubs, three gyms.
about.p2: For half a year, I put off starting to work out.
about.p3: Every morning I told myself: today's the day. Then I'd open my browser, find the three gyms, zero useful info, one phone number nobody picked up — and close the tab. Tomorrow.
about.p4: When I finally walked through the door, I paid cash because they didn't take cards. Nobody mentioned that anywhere. Around me were guys who'd been training there for ten years — laughing, greeting each other by name. And I stood in the doorway in my H&M t-shirt, clueless. I asked basic questions and felt like an idiot. Everyone knew each other; I knew no one. Everyone knew where everything was; I didn't.
about.p5: I left red-faced. Didn't go back for a month.
about.p6: And you know what the worst part was? The second week I forgot my cash. I drove over, turned around in the doorway, found a cash machine on the other side of town — and when I got back, it was closed. Just closed. No warning.
about.p7: Deciding to go to the gym for the first time in your life is one of the hardest decisions. And that day I said to myself: this can't be this hard.
about.p8: All I wanted was one thing. To buy a drop-in pass from my phone. To know they'd let me in. To arrive prepared. No cash, no red face, no walk of shame back to the car.
about.pullQuote: IRONMAP was born from that walk-away moment.
about.p9: Today we have 780 gyms in 190 cities. We add more every week. One day we want to be the place where you find every sport in the Czech Republic — from lifting to yoga to climbing walls. And where you can buy your entry before you start the car.
about.p10: So that first step doesn't hurt. For anyone.
